document_ratings.weight_rationale: Canonical founding document; concentrated evidence for sacred-object, sacrificial pledge, and enemy-construction dimensions.
document_ratings.rationale: The Declaration figures liberty as an unalienable sacred object, pledges lives and fortunes as founding sacrifice, and constructs the king as a systematic bringer of destruction and death through the enumerated grievances list. Historical alignment is strong: the document triggered the war and is formally enacted as the founding charter.

document_ratings.weight_rationale: Principal pamphlet corpus source (53 of 61 CMT instances); supplies the dominant body, sacrifice, slavery-bondage, and providence clusters.
document_ratings.rationale: Common Sense saturates the corpus with sacrifice, birth-generation, and slavery-bondage metaphors. The people-republic cluster (26 instances) constitutes the sacred collective object. Britain/monarchy is figured as bringer of fire, sword, and bondage. Historical alignment is contextually corroborated as a mass-mobilization text preceding the war, but it is rhetorical rather than a formal-enactment document.

limitations: Scores are provisional and based on reviewed am-rev pilot evidence (61 instances, Jefferson Declaration and Common Sense only).
Washington Orders documents have 0 metaphorical instances in the current review due to a known MIPVU overwrite issue (tech debt #42); re-review may alter scores.
Full-corpus MIPVU review and reliability adjudication are pending.
Historical alignment notes require publication-grade citations.
